pattern: With only five cards the ranking collapses to two independent frequency checks: all suits equal, else the largest rank multiplicity decides between three-of-a-kind, pair, and high card.
hints: A flush only needs every suit to be identical, so test that first since it outranks everything else.
Otherwise the best hand is decided purely by the most frequent rank: three or more, exactly two, or all distinct.
If all five suits match return Flush; else take the maximum rank count and map 3+ to Three of a Kind, 2 to Pair, 1 to High Card.

edge_inputs.description: all suits identical gives a flush
four of one rank counts as three of a kind
all distinct ranks and mixed suits is high card
three of a kind beats a coexisting pair
two separate pairs still rank only as Pair

stress.description: random valid 5-card hands with no duplicate (rank,suit)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    suits_pool = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd']
    cards = [(r, s) for r in range(1, 14) for s in suits_pool]
    rng.shuffle(cards)
    chosen = cards[:5]
    ranks = [c[0] for c in chosen]
    suits = [c[1] for c in chosen]
    return [ranks, suits]
stress.note: guarantees distinct (rank,suit) pairs as the problem requires
